instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `with-logging` that takes a label (any literal — keyword, symbol, or string) and a body. The expansion runs the body inside a PROGN, preceded by `(format t "[enter ~A]~%" 'label)` and followed by `(format t "[exit ~A]~%" 'label)`. The label is quoted in the expansion so it appears verbatim in the trace. Useful for ad-hoc tracing without the full TRACE machinery.
